content.problemStatement:
Chef is learning about **absolute** MEX sequences.

A sequence AAA of NNN integers is said to be an **absolute** MEX sequence, if the following property holds for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1\le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N:


‚ą£Ai‚ąíMEX(A1,A2,‚Ä¶,Ai)‚ą£‚Č§1|A_i - \texttt{MEX} (A_1, A_2, \dots, A_i)| \leq 1‚ą£Ai‚Äč‚ąíMEX(A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ai‚Äč)‚ą£‚Č§1
Chef has two integers NNN and MMM. Find the total number of **absolute** MEX sequences AAA, of length NNN, such that 0‚Č§Ai‚Č§M0 \leq A_i \leq M0‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§M.

Since answer can be huge, output the answer modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

Note:


‚ą£X‚ą£|X|‚ą£X‚ą£ denotes the absolute value of XXX. For example, ‚ą£4‚ą£=4|4| = 4‚ą£4‚ą£=4 and ‚ą£‚ąí7‚ą£=7|-7| = 7‚ą£‚ąí7‚ą£=7.
MEX(A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN)\texttt{MEX} (A_1, A_2, \dots, A_N)MEX(A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč) denotes the MEX of the sequence [A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN][A_1, A_2, \dots, A_N][A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč]. For example, MEX(2,2,1)=0\texttt{MEX}(2,2,1)=0MEX(2,2,1)=0, and MEX(3,1,0,1)=2\texttt{MEX}(3, 1, 0, 1)=2MEX(3,1,0,1)=2.

Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then, the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers NNN and MMM, as mentioned in statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line, the total number of **absolute** MEX sequences satisfying the condition, modulo 109+710^9+7109+7 .
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1062 \cdot 10^62‚čÖ106
The sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1062 \cdot 10^62‚čÖ106
